title: Create one-way project skill connection workflow
summary: Create a foundry workflow, likely as a skill derived from initiate-task, that connects an external project to the foundry as a one-way read-only skill source so the foundry can run approved project skills while the source project remains isolated, and foundry-run outputs are stored as foundry-owned records instead of project-owned testing or usage artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: grilling identifies the source project, its skill surface, and the operator job for connecting it to the foundry
the workflow defines a one-way connection contract where the foundry can read or invoke approved project skills without writing into the source project
the workflow preserves project isolation and prevents foundry tracker state, generated artifacts, or workflow mutations from infecting the connected project
foundry-run records for connected project skills are stored under projects/workflow-foundry/artifacts/connected-runs/<source-project-slug>/<run-id>/ and labeled as foundry-owned
the connection discovers all source project skill paths by default, then compares the connected project against the foundry before deciding how each skill should be integrated or run

the comparison produces a foundry-owned integration report that classifies each discovered source skill as run-as-is, needs-foundry-wrapper, duplicates-foundry-skill, conflicts-with-foundry-rule, or do-not-run
no source project is connected, and no foundry-owned wrapper or adapter is created, without explicit operator approval for that integration
the operator-facing entrypoint remains initiate-task, with explicit connection intent activating additional connection workflow behavior rather than a separate primary skill

connection intent can be expressed either with structured fields such as target:connection source_project:<slug-or-path> or with clear natural language that says the initiated task is for connecting or integrating a project's skills into the foundry
the workflow defines one foundry-owned connection record per source project, with every discovered skill marked as connected or not connected and classified by the integration report

project-level connection records live under projects/workflow-foundry/artifacts/connections/<source-project-slug>.json while individual run outputs remain under projects/workflow-foundry/artifacts/connected-runs/<source-project-slug>/<run-id>/
the derived task flow preserves initiate-task preload, phase guard, Matt Pocock phase handling, and ECC concepts
an eval gate or fixture proves only approved connected skills are discoverable and that source-project files remain unchanged

context_snapshot.summary: Load workflow-foundry state, every non-done workflow-foundry task, Matt/ECC references, and initiate-task before shaping the one-way project skill connection workflow.

session_log.event: Created intake task after user invoked initiate-task for a one-way project skill connection workflow where the foundry can use approved project skills while the source project remains isolated.
Moved to grilling after user asked to grill the one-way project skill connection workflow.

Resolved first boundary: connected project skill source files are read-only for the foundry; the foundry may run approved skills, but any files or records produced by a foundry run must be stored and labeled as foundry-owned rather than as source-project testing or usage artifacts.

Resolved foundry-owned run storage: connected project skill run records should live under projects/workflow-foundry/artifacts/connected-runs/<source-project-slug>/<run-id>/, separate from source-project testing sessions or project-owned usage artifacts.

Resolved discovery mode: connected projects should expose all skill paths to the foundry by default, but integration must compare the source project against the foundry so skills are connected intentionally rather than through a narrow allowlist or blind import.

Resolved comparison output: the foundry-owned integration report should classify every discovered source skill as run-as-is, needs-foundry-wrapper, duplicates-foundry-skill, conflicts-with-foundry-rule, or do-not-run.

Resolved integration approval gate: source project connection should be rare and explicit; the foundry must not connect a project or create a foundry-owned wrapper or adapter unless the operator deliberately approves that integration.

Resolved connection record granularity: create one foundry-owned connection record for the whole source project, and record inside it which discovered skills are connected or not connected along with their comparison classification.

Resolved connection storage: keep project-level connection records under projects/workflow-foundry/artifacts/connections/<source-project-slug>.json, separate from per-run outputs under projects/workflow-foundry/artifacts/connected-runs/<source-project-slug>/<run-id>/.

Resolved operator entrypoint: keep initiate-task as the primary user-facing command, and activate connection-specific behavior only when the operator explicitly says the initiated task is for connecting a project or connection integration.

Resolved invocation shape: support a structured initiate-task form such as target:connection source_project:<slug-or-path>, while also accepting clear natural-language requests that explicitly say the task is for connecting or integrating a project's skills into the foundry.
